source: Monster of the Week: Slayer's Survival Kit (EHP0073)
note: Team playbooks are shared resources for the whole group, not individual hunters. One team playbook is active per campaign. PORTAL uses Research Lab.

team_playbooks.name: Research Lab
team_playbooks.description: The hunters are staff and residents of a research lab, making them uniquely equipped to handle fringe science problems in the world around them.
team_playbooks.flavour_note: Magic-focused hunters may need tweaking — frame their powers in a parapsychological way.
getting_started: Pick your style (and associated advancement question).
Pick a starting team enemy, ally, move, and assets.
Join the group message server and brainstorm.

on_fill: Make the next mystery about your team enemy, giving you a chance to interfere in their plans.

styles.name: Middle of Nowhere
styles.description: There's nothing around you but a small town and weird stuff going on.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we stop a threat from spreading beyond the town?

styles.name: 0 Days Since Last Incident
styles.description: There are constantly accidents here. At least, you hope they're accidents.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we uncover or fix something our lab caused?

styles.name: Explorers
styles.description: Your team and facility seek out and send expeditions to study strange events.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we discover something that amazed the scientific community?

styles.name: Hazardous Research
styles.description: Your facility has discovered things with as much potential for evil as good.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we find safe and beneficial applications for the dangerous things we learned about?

styles.name: Action Science
styles.description: It's not real research if it can't bite you — or destroy the world!
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we defeat extreme danger with science?

styles.name: Custom
styles.description: Define your own team story and end-of-session question.
styles.end_of_session_question: FILL_FROM_SESSION

enemies: Dr J. Douglas, the former lab director who turned out to be evil.
Canopy Research, a shadowy corporation turning people into monsters.
Thaphaeth, an alien warlord trying to establish a beachhead on our world.
The mothman, an extra-dimensional entity sowing chaos.
All the aliens.
Muramasa Heavy Industries, a weapons company.
Custom: FILL_FROM_SESSION

allies.name: Kana Harlison
allies.description: A cat burglar with a taste for helping out in a crisis. Stealth is their deal, but they can't resist stealing things if they get a chance.

allies.name: Dalia Amin
allies.description: A local community leader. They'll get cooperation from locals, whether it's taking cover or getting some manual labour done. They want you to look after the community, too.

allies.name: Sergeant Qeb Gomez
allies.description: Local law enforcement. He can keep the team aware of what the cops plan to do, and act as your liaison.

allies.name: CAMBELL (sic), the lab AI
allies.description: Handles most background computation and maintenance tasks. Exceptionally good at creating virtual scenarios to test theories.
allies.portal_note: Intentional single-L spelling in source. In the PORTAL campaign this maps directly to CAMPBELL — the team's lab AI briefing system.

allies.name: Dr Yos Sokha
allies.description: A former colleague. Can provide research and data from different teams and different viewpoints.

allies.name: Questerr and Wrexy
allies.description: Diplomats from a friendly alien civilisation. They're also concerned about your ongoing troubles, and sometimes provide you with rare resources or advanced technology.

allies.name: Custom
allies.description: FILL_FROM_SESSION

moves.name: Scientific Method
moves.description: When the team investigates a mystery, they can ask one of the following questions in addition to the usual result.
choices: Which of our ideas is wrong?
What weird energies or materials are present?

moves.name: Trial and Error
moves.description: When a team member rolls a 10+ on kick some ass or protect someone, you can choose to ask a question from investigate a mystery in addition to your normal option.

moves.name: Repurpose Old Junk
moves.description: Once per session, ignore all but one requirement for weird science or any similar move.

moves.name: Pattern Recognition
moves.description: Once per session, after a team member successfully reads a bad situation, they may ask a follow-up question about ensuring or preventing a specific outcome.
moves.choices_note: Fill in: 'How do I ensure or prevent _______ happening?'

moves.name: Button Mashing
moves.description: When presented with technology beyond your experience, work together to study it. Each team member who helps out gets +1 forward.

moves.name: Something Different
moves.description: Take a move from another team playbook.

moves.name: Change Team Style
moves.description: Pick a new team style. You now use that one instead of the one you first chose.

mandatory.name: The Lab
mandatory.description: A well-stocked and extensive laboratory complex that can be used for weird science and high-tech big magic, and has space for all your gear and instruments. There's even some crash space for when you need to pull an all-nighter.

pick_one.name: Sensor Array
pick_one.description: Instruments allowing you to keep track of weird phenomena in the area.

pick_one.name: Cutting-Edge Analysis
pick_one.description: Access to any current device (no matter how expensive).

pick_one.name: Manufacturing Bays
pick_one.description: A blend of 3D printers and old-school assembly lines. You can build any test device or prototype you want (but no mass production).

pick_one.name: Medical Bay
pick_one.description: High-tech medical facilities equivalent to staying in a hospital.

pick_one.name: Unobtanium
pick_one.description: Access to a unique resource, allowing extreme science. Pick a name and use for the resource, and a research area that it helps you with. All work in that area is enhanced.

pick_one.name: More Unobtanium
pick_one.description: Select another special resource and the research area it helps with.

pick_one.name: Even More Unobtanium
pick_one.description: Select another special resource and the research area it helps with.

pick_one.name: Vehicle Bay
pick_one.description: The lab has a small fleet of vehicles. Describe a standard sort of ground vehicle and a single air vehicle.

pick_one.name: Housing
pick_one.description: Each team member has comfortable and secure housing, either a small house or a comfortable condo. Loaded with tech.

pick_one.name: Containment
pick_one.description: The lab has facilities to keep live creatures or dangerous materials securely contained.

player_choices.notes: Style confirmed: Action Science (end-of-session question: 'Did we defeat extreme danger with science?'). Starting move defaulting to Scientific Method per book recommendation. Enemy, ally, and asset picks need recording from session 01.

keeper_notes.tone: Fieldwork and lab work should both feel important. 'You found a sample you need to get back to the lab' is a valid investigate-a-mystery answer. Lab equipment can be a requirement for weird science or science-based big magic.
variety_tips: Set a mystery entirely within the lab — enemies lay siege to the facility.
Cut the team off from their lab entirely via natural disaster or sinister plot.
Let hunter precautions pay off later when the same threat returns.

team_playbooks.name: Artifact Collectors
team_playbooks.description: The hunters are collectors, archivists, or custodians of dangerous supernatural objects. The artefacts find them as much as they find the artefacts.
team_playbooks.keeper_note: Be careful about narratives that lean into colonial acquisition or theft. Discuss with your group.

styles.name: Vault of Curses
styles.description: Everything you hold is dangerous. Containment is survival.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we prevent an artefact from causing catastrophic harm?

styles.name: Arcane Study
styles.description: Understanding is your goal. You collect to learn.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we learn something significant about a supernatural object?

styles.name: Unworthy Hands
styles.description: You take these things away from people who'd misuse them.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we recover something from someone who had no right to it?

styles.name: Safe Storage
styles.description: You're custodians, not researchers. Keeping things secure is the job.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we prevent a breach or loss from our collection?

styles.name: My Precious
styles.description: You know some of you are getting too attached. That's a problem.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did someone resist the pull of an artefact they really wanted?

styles.name: Custom
styles.description: Define your own team story.
styles.end_of_session_question: FILL_FROM_SESSION

enemies: A famous museum whose acquisitions are shadier than they look
Turner Jamison, a private collector with no ethics
Jyika, a naga who considers your collection theirs
Zeus (yes, that one), who wants a specific artefact back
A criminal gang that moves supernatural contraband
Custom: FILL_FROM_SESSION

allies.name: Felis Quinn
allies.description: A fence and appraiser who knows what things are worth and what they do.

allies.name: Marie Allison
allies.description: A retired hunter who now works as a researcher for your team.

allies.name: Farrok Andant
allies.description: A customs officer who looks the other way for you — mostly.

allies.name: Nalani
allies.description: A protective spirit bound to one of your artefacts, grudgingly cooperative.

allies.name: Zof Enarra
allies.description: A dimensional entity who helps catalogue objects from their plane of origin.

allies.name: Robot Pants Studios
allies.description: A tech startup that builds your containment hardware. Don't ask why the name.

allies.name: Custom
allies.description: FILL_FROM_SESSION

moves.name: Check the Manifest
moves.description: When you investigate a supernatural object, roll +Sharp. On 10+: you know exactly what it is, what it does, and how to contain or destroy it. On 7–9: you know two of the three.

moves.name: I Smell Fudge
moves.description: When you detect a fake or misidentified artefact, you know. Ask the Keeper one question about the real item's location or nature.

moves.name: Where in the World?
moves.description: Once per session, trace any supernatural object's current location, no matter where it is.

moves.name: Quicktime
moves.description: When you need to get an artefact somewhere fast, it gets there — complications come later, not now.

moves.name: You're Not Worthy
moves.description: When you deny someone access to an artefact by force or argument, take +1 ongoing for the scene.

moves.name: Something Different
moves.description: Take a move from another team playbook.

moves.name: Change Team Style
moves.description: Pick a new team style. You now use that one instead of the one you first chose.

options.name: Mobile Base
options.description: A vehicle large enough to transport and store artefacts securely.

options.name: Mobile Base (upgrade)
options.description: A second or upgraded mobile base.

options.name: Smuggler Connections
options.description: People who can move items across borders without questions.

options.name: Approved Artifacts
options.description: A selection of supernatural items sanctioned for your team's use.

options.name: Approved Artifacts (more)
options.description: Additional sanctioned items.

options.name: Transportation
options.description: Reliable vehicles for fieldwork.

options.name: Halls of Academia
options.description: University or museum connections for research and authentication.

options.name: Vault
options.description: Secure supernaturally-reinforced storage.

options.name: Vault (expanded)
options.description: A second or expanded vault.

options.name: Headquarters
options.description: A fixed base of operations.

options.name: Front Business
options.description: A legitimate business that explains your activities.

options.name: Official Connections
options.description: Contacts in government, law enforcement, or customs.

options.name: Custom
options.description: FILL_FROM_SESSION

team_playbooks.name: Coven
team_playbooks.description: The hunters are a magical group united by shared power and a mark of kinship. Each carries something of the supernatural, and together that's more than the sum of its parts.

mark_of_kinship.note: Each hunter gains a Mark of Kinship when they join this team. Pick one type per hunter:
options: Gain a curse (as Monstrous) and one Monstrous move
Gain 2 rotes (as Initiate) and a Temptation
Gain one Spooky move and 2 Dark Secrets
Gain the Unknown Heritage tag system (as Changeling)

styles.name: Villains to Heroes
styles.description: You used to be the threat. Now you're the solution.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we use our power for protection rather than self-interest?

styles.name: Superheroes with Fangs
styles.description: Embrace the monster aesthetic. You protect the world anyway.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we protect someone who feared us?

styles.name: Forgotten History
styles.description: Your coven has roots going back further than anyone remembers.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we uncover or honour something from our coven's past?

styles.name: Ulterior Motives
styles.description: You're not sure everyone in the group has the same agenda.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we keep the coven together despite conflicting interests?

styles.name: Custom
styles.description: Define your own.
styles.end_of_session_question: FILL_FROM_SESSION

moves.name: Bound in Blood
moves.description: When you spend 1-harm to help an ally, you can assist them from any distance, not just when nearby.

moves.name: How Awful Goodness Is
moves.description: When one of you makes a hard moral choice that costs them personally, the whole coven marks experience.

moves.name: Our Strength Is the Pack
moves.description: When three or more coven members work together on a roll, add +1 to the result on top of any help bonuses.

moves.name: Laws of Hospitality
moves.description: Creatures and entities that follow supernatural codes of conduct cannot directly attack you while you observe those codes. Breaks when you violate them.

moves.name: Bell, Book and Candle
moves.description: Once per session, perform a binding or banishment without the usual requirements for use magic.

moves.name: Something Different
moves.description: Take a move from another team playbook.

moves.name: Change Team Style
moves.description: Pick a new team style.

options.name: Headquarters
options.description: A base of operations with ritual space.

options.name: Sanctuaries
options.description: Safe locations warded against supernatural intrusion.

options.name: Scrying Device
options.description: A tool for remote observation.

options.name: Scrying Device (upgraded)
options.description: A more powerful or additional scrying tool.

options.name: Wards and Barriers
options.description: Protective workings that make your base hard to attack supernaturally.

options.name: Wards and Barriers (expanded)
options.description: Wards extended to additional locations.

options.name: Archive
options.description: A library of occult knowledge and grimoires.

options.name: Armoury
options.description: Supernaturally-enhanced weapons and protective gear.

options.name: Leyline Map
options.description: Detailed knowledge of local supernatural geography.

options.name: Leyline Map (expanded)
options.description: Regional or global leyline knowledge.

options.name: Ritual Cupboard
options.description: A well-stocked supply of ritual components.

options.name: Custom
options.description: FILL_FROM_SESSION

team_playbooks.name: Escaped Experiments
team_playbooks.description: You were made, modified, or created by a project called The Project. You got out. Now you're dealing with the world while The Project is still out there.

scrubs_vs_gowns.note: Each hunter chooses a role: Scrub (Project staff who turned) or Gown (Project subject who escaped).
scrub_abilities: Access to Project protocols and jargon
gown_abilities: One modification from The Project (treat as a Monstrous move)
Better at resisting Project recapture attempts
Others in your position recognise you as one of them

styles.name: Hostile Acquisition
styles.description: The Project is coming for you. You're going to them first.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we strike a meaningful blow against The Project?

styles.name: Scales from the Eyes
styles.description: You're learning the truth of what was done to you.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we uncover something true about The Project or ourselves?

styles.name: Never Asked for This
styles.description: You want a normal life. That's not possible. You're handling it.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did someone manage to live as a person, not a weapon, even briefly?

styles.name: Custom
styles.description: FILL_FROM_SESSION
styles.end_of_session_question: FILL_FROM_SESSION

allies.name: Maya Kaseem
allies.description: A journalist documenting Project abuses. Brave and reckless.

allies.name: Britt Ravyn
allies.description: A security specialist who helped you escape. Still owes you, or you still owe them.

allies.name: Oracle Chantesuta
allies.description: A psychic who can sometimes predict Project movements.

allies.name: Little Sister
allies.description: An AI that was also a Project creation. Now autonomous and on your side. Mostly.

allies.name: Raisa Neri
allies.description: A lawyer who specialises in illegal human experimentation cases.

allies.name: Jay 'Wage' Donohue
allies.description: A black-market supplier who doesn't ask questions about what you need or why.

allies.name: Custom
allies.description: FILL_FROM_SESSION

moves.name: Ethical Concerns
moves.description: When you investigate something related to human experimentation or supernatural modification, you may ask two extra questions beyond the normal result.

moves.name: Camera Shy
moves.description: You cannot be captured on film, recorded, or tracked electronically unless you choose to allow it.

moves.name: Strike Back
moves.description: When you fight back against the Project or their agents, take +1 ongoing for the scene.

moves.name: Shared Pain
moves.description: When any team member takes harm, all members may share it equally (round down per person). Must be agreed before the harm is dealt.

moves.name: Cross-Training
moves.description: Each team member may take one move from another team member's playbook once.

moves.name: Something Different
moves.description: Take a move from another team playbook.

moves.name: Change Team Style
moves.description: Pick a new team style.

options.name: Caches
options.description: Hidden supply caches throughout the area.

options.name: Bolt-Hole
options.description: An emergency refuge the Project doesn't know about.

options.name: Headquarters
options.description: A base of operations.

options.name: Under the Table
options.description: Access to black-market supplies and off-the-books services.

options.name: Improvised Clinic
options.description: Medical facilities that can treat modified physiology.

options.name: Improvised Clinic (upgraded)
options.description: Better-equipped clinic.

options.name: Armoury
options.description: Weapons and gear.

options.name: Transportation
options.description: Reliable vehicles.

options.name: Mobile Base
options.description: A vehicle that doubles as a headquarters.

options.name: Mobile Base (expanded)
options.description: A larger or better-equipped mobile base.

options.name: Custom
options.description: FILL_FROM_SESSION

team_playbooks.name: Good Monsters
team_playbooks.description: Every hunter is a monster of some kind. You've made a choice about what to do with that. So far.

monster_sides.note: Each hunter plays their main playbook plus a Monstrous overlay. Pick a curse and one natural attack (mandatory for all). Optionally, one hunter may be fully human instead.
monster_sides.human_option: A human hunter gets 1 extra Luck box. If they become a monster during play, they lose that Luck box permanently.

styles.name: Walk the Line
styles.description: You're monsters who choose every day not to be. That choice is everything.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did everyone resist their monstrous nature when it counted?

styles.name: Outcasts
styles.description: Humans won't have you. Monsters don't trust you. You have each other.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we help each other when the world pushed back?

styles.name: Being Human
styles.description: You're learning what humanity means from the outside.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did someone do something that felt genuinely human?

styles.name: On the Inside
styles.description: You work within monster society to make it better.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we change something about how monsters and humans interact?

styles.name: Custom
styles.description: FILL_FROM_SESSION
styles.end_of_session_question: FILL_FROM_SESSION

extra_advanced_improvements: Free yourself from your curse — lose 1 Weird
Turn evil (again) — retire this character as a Keeper threat

moves.name: Normal Human Activity
moves.description: When you attempt to pass off supernatural events or your own monstrous behaviour as mundane, roll +Cool. On 10+: completely convincing, no one looks further. On 7–9: convincing for now. Miss: someone knows.

moves.name: Mark of Evil
moves.description: Other supernatural entities recognise you as kin. Once per session, call on that recognition to get information, safe passage, or assistance from a monster.

moves.name: Rite of Empowerment
moves.description: Once per session, perform a brief ritual to give one team member +1 forward on their next roll.

moves.name: Too Many Monsters!
moves.description: When you face a monster that shares your type, you understand them instinctively. Take +1 ongoing and ask one free question about their nature.

moves.name: Mystic Radio
moves.description: The team can communicate supernaturally across any distance, once per session without any gear.

moves.name: Something Different
moves.description: Take a move from another team playbook.

moves.name: Change Team Style
moves.description: Pick a new team style.

options.name: Mobile Base
options.description: A vehicle-based headquarters.

options.name: Mobile Base (expanded)
options.description: Larger or better-equipped mobile base.

options.name: Individual Transport
options.description: Each team member has their own vehicle.

options.name: Normality Wardings
options.description: Magical disguises that make you read as human to supernatural detection.

options.name: Headquarters
options.description: A fixed base that accepts monsters.

options.name: Headquarters (expanded)
options.description: Larger or better-equipped headquarters.

options.name: Mystical Workroom
options.description: A space set up for supernatural work.

options.name: Mystical Workroom (expanded)
options.description: A more powerful workroom.

options.name: Workshop
options.description: Tools and space for crafting and repair.

options.name: Disguises
options.description: Gear and techniques for passing as human.

options.name: Self-Management Setup
options.description: Resources to manage your monstrous needs — feeding, containment, rituals.

options.name: Map of the Moonlight Pathways
options.description: Knowledge of supernatural routes between locations, usable by those with Weird powers.

options.name: Custom
options.description: FILL_FROM_SESSION

team_playbooks.name: Medical Team
team_playbooks.description: The hunters work from a medical facility. They patch up people others can't explain, treat things no textbook covers, and try not to lose too many patients.

styles.name: Morgue
styles.description: Most of what you deal with comes in on a slab.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we uncover the cause of a supernatural death?

styles.name: All-Hours Clinic
styles.description: You're open when no one else is, for people with nowhere else to go.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we help someone who had no other options?

styles.name: General Practitioner
styles.description: You're embedded in a community. You see everything.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we protect our patients' trust?

styles.name: Hospital
styles.description: Big institution. Politics. But also the best equipment.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we keep the supernatural out of public records?

styles.name: Medical School
styles.description: Teaching the next generation — carefully.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we teach something useful without revealing too much?

styles.name: Custom
styles.description: FILL_FROM_SESSION
styles.end_of_session_question: FILL_FROM_SESSION

enemies: The Riddleman Group, a pharmaceutical company with supernatural interests
The Rijpma family, who blame you for a death
A spirit of death that resents your interference
Dr Horner, a colleague who knows too much and has the wrong values
The Quiet Order, who consider your work a violation of natural law
All the mummies (don't ask)
Custom: FILL_FROM_SESSION

allies.name: Ishey Wei
allies.description: A paramedic who handles calls that don't make it into the official report.

allies.name: Dr Mel Yvette
allies.description: A specialist who consults on impossible cases. Very discreet.

allies.name: Vir Loleri
allies.description: A hospital administrator who keeps your funding and paperwork in order.

allies.name: Luna Star
allies.description: A herbalist and alternative practitioner who covers things modern medicine can't explain.

allies.name: Sapfo Hadjiti
allies.description: A forensic pathologist who knows when something isn't natural cause of death.

allies.name: Harry Michaels
allies.description: A patient who survived something impossible and now helps you in gratitude.

allies.name: Custom
allies.description: FILL_FROM_SESSION

moves.name: Medical Professional
moves.description: When you treat someone's injuries, they heal +1 harm beyond the normal amount.

moves.name: House Diagnostics
moves.description: When you examine a patient showing impossible symptoms, ask two questions from the investigate a mystery list for free.

moves.name: Code Blue!
moves.description: Once per session, stabilise someone who should be dead. They survive until the end of the mystery, then the Keeper decides their fate.

moves.name: I'm Not a Miracle Worker
moves.description: When you attempt something medically impossible and fail, the failure is clean — no collateral damage, no making things worse.

moves.name: Bedside Manner
moves.description: When you calm or reassure a frightened patient or bystander, roll +Charm. On 10+: they tell you everything and are fully cooperative. On 7–9: they tell you what they think you need to know.

moves.name: Something Different
moves.description: Take a move from another team playbook.

moves.name: Change Team Style
moves.description: Pick a new team style.

mandatory.name: Infirmary
mandatory.description: A full professional medical facility. Can treat any injury, human or supernatural. Equivalent to a hospital stay for recovery purposes.

options.name: Ambulance Fleet
options.description: Vehicles for emergency response.

options.name: Cutting-Edge Diagnostics
options.description: Equipment that can identify supernatural conditions alongside mundane ones.

options.name: Cutting-Edge Diagnostics (expanded)
options.description: More advanced or specialised diagnostic tools.

options.name: Medical Database
options.description: A private database of supernatural conditions and treatments.

options.name: Local Support
options.description: Community connections who provide supplies, information, and cover.

options.name: Housing
options.description: Staff accommodation on-site or nearby.

options.name: Individual Transport
options.description: Personal vehicles for each team member.

options.name: Experimental Medicine
options.description: Access to treatments that don't officially exist yet.

options.name: Experimental Medicine (expanded)
options.description: A wider range of experimental treatments.

options.name: Funding
options.description: Grants, private donations, or other reliable income streams.

options.name: Custom
options.description: FILL_FROM_SESSION

team_playbooks.name: Mundane Monstrosities
team_playbooks.description: You're teenagers or young adults with minor supernatural abilities you were born with. You didn't choose this. The monsters didn't get the memo.

upbringing.note: Choose one upbringing that describes your community.

options.name: Unseen Community
options.description: A hidden neighbourhood where your kind has always lived.

options.name: Precocious Youth
options.description: School-age hunters dealing with monsters between classes.

options.name: Protected and Subsidised
options.description: Someone funds and protects you. You don't fully understand why.

options.name: Scattered Individuals
options.description: You found each other despite having no shared community.

options.name: Secret Haven
options.description: You have a place that's yours, hidden from the world.

options.name: Weird Town
options.description: The whole town is strange. You're just the ones who do something about it.

minor_weirdness.note: Each hunter starts with one Minor Weirdness: one move from another playbook (Artifact, Monstrous moves, Guardian, Inhuman Talent, Jinx/Sight, Soothe, Third Eye). These represent powers they were born with or that manifested naturally.

styles.name: Life Interrupted
styles.description: You had plans. Monsters got in the way.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did someone manage to do something normal despite everything?

styles.name: Playtime's Over
styles.description: You used to think this was a game. Not anymore.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we take the threat seriously even when it was hard?

styles.name: A Calling
styles.description: You feel this is what you're supposed to do.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we answer the call when it mattered?

styles.name: Fitting In
styles.description: You're trying to seem normal. You're failing.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did someone manage to seem completely normal to someone important to them?

styles.name: Hush
styles.description: Secrecy is everything. No one can know.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we keep our activities completely hidden?

styles.name: Custom
styles.description: FILL_FROM_SESSION
styles.end_of_session_question: FILL_FROM_SESSION

enemies: Karen and Kevin, suburban parents who have noticed something is wrong
SPLEEN, a government agency that monitors minor paranormal activity in young people
The Council of Elder Entities, who do not approve of unsupervised youth involvement
All the realtors (they know what houses are built on)
A supernatural clique at school who see you as competition
Helen and Jeb Derry, who saw something they shouldn't have
Janika Smirnov, who wants to recruit you for something much more serious
Custom: FILL_FROM_SESSION

allies.name: Agent Maury DeWhit
allies.description: A government agent who covers for you instead of reporting you. So far.

allies.name: Stefan Knight
allies.description: An older hunter who mentors the group. Reluctantly.

allies.name: Kim Chin-Mae
allies.description: A classmate who figured it out and is remarkably chill about it.

allies.name: Ash Lawrence
allies.description: A local eccentric with an impressive occult library and no parental supervision.

allies.name: Jerek Jaafar
allies.description: Someone's older sibling who drives and doesn't ask questions.

allies.name: Alytari Gandega
allies.description: A friendly supernatural entity who acts as an informal guide.

allies.name: Custom
allies.description: FILL_FROM_SESSION

moves.name: Too Normal to Be Weird
moves.description: When you act completely confident and mundane, supernatural entities find it harder to target you. Take +1 armour against supernatural attacks as long as you haven't used your Minor Weirdness in the scene.

moves.name: Casual Magic
moves.description: When you use your Minor Weirdness in a way that looks completely accidental or normal, no one suspects it was intentional.

moves.name: Authentic Witchiness
moves.description: When you lean into looking weird and supernatural, roll +Weird. On 10+: actual supernatural entities treat you as a peer. On 7–9: they're curious rather than hostile. Miss: you attract the wrong kind of attention.

moves.name: Curse Remedies
moves.description: Once per session, attempt to lift a curse or minor supernatural affliction without the usual use magic requirements.

moves.name: Just Folks
moves.description: When you blend into a crowd or community setting, you're effectively invisible to supernatural detection.

moves.name: Something Different
moves.description: Take a move from another team playbook.

moves.name: Change Team Style
moves.description: Pick a new team style.

options.name: Tutorial Focus
options.description: A private space to practice and develop powers safely.

options.name: Housing
options.description: Somewhere to crash that's safe and private.

options.name: Magical Hangout
options.description: A location with ambient supernatural energy that helps with magical work.

options.name: Magical Hangout (expanded)
options.description: A more powerful or extensive magical location.

options.name: Supernatural Communications
options.description: A way to communicate magically — sigils, crystals, or a shared dream space.

options.name: Ritual Ingredients
options.description: Stockpile of components for supernatural workings.

options.name: Protective Amulets
options.description: One per team member. +1 armour against supernatural attacks.

options.name: Protective Amulets (upgraded)
options.description: Better amulets or more of them.

options.name: Transportation
options.description: A reliable way to get around.

options.name: The Spirit-Web
options.description: A network of local spirits who provide information and minor assistance.

options.name: Clubhouse
options.description: A place that's yours, secret, and comfortable.

options.name: Custom
options.description: FILL_FROM_SESSION

team_playbooks.name: Radio Station
team_playbooks.description: The hunters run a radio station. They broadcast to a community. The station has become a hub for the supernatural whether they wanted it to or not.

options: College station with a student listener base
Remote village served by a low-power transmitter
Scattered townships with no other local media
A big city with thousands of listeners
A global audience via internet streaming

styles.name: Casual Absurdity
styles.description: You report on the impossible with the tone of a traffic update.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we inform the public about something impossible without causing panic?

styles.name: Shock and Terror
styles.description: Horror is your format. The real monsters make it easy.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we use our platform to expose genuine supernatural terror?

styles.name: Exuberance
styles.description: You love this. The monsters are the best story you've ever covered.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we get the story despite everything?

styles.name: Custom
styles.description: FILL_FROM_SESSION
styles.end_of_session_question: FILL_FROM_SESSION

enemies: Ghost in the Machine — a supernatural entity that has been in your signal for years
'A Frowning Devil' — a cult that listens very carefully to your broadcasts
Ulmer Broadcasting Interests — they want your frequency
The Looming Stalker — a supernatural entity fixated on one of your DJs
Savidan — a rival broadcaster with deeply suspicious ratings success
Custom: FILL_FROM_SESSION

allies.name: Natalya Angelique
allies.description: A loyal listener who has become a reliable field source.

allies.name: Executive Producer Fujita Tarō
allies.description: Your EP. Knows more about the supernatural than they let on.

allies.name: International Hunting Group of Awesome
allies.description: A network of hunters that formed around your signal. They're enthusiastic.

allies.name: Pearl Aiza
allies.description: A sound engineer who can manipulate recordings in ways that shouldn't be possible.

allies.name: Levi Shalom
allies.description: A music archivist who knows which songs are actually dangerous to play.

allies.name: Hibo Salah
allies.description: A community liaison who makes sure your broadcasts help rather than panic.

allies.name: Custom
allies.description: FILL_FROM_SESSION

moves.name: I'll Get You through This
moves.description: When you broadcast directly to someone in danger, roll +Cool. On 10+: they take +2 forward and feel calm. On 7–9: they take +1 forward. You don't need to be physically present.

moves.name: Don't Touch that Dial
moves.description: When you use your broadcast to compel a supernatural entity to stop and listen, roll +Weird. On 10+: it's frozen for the scene. On 7–9: it's distracted and slowed. Miss: it's attracted rather than distracted.

moves.name: Our Next Caller
moves.description: Once per session, a listener calls in with exactly the information you needed. The Keeper decides who and why.

moves.name: A Word from Our Sponsors
moves.description: When you need resources quickly, roll +Charm. On 10+: a sponsor or listener provides what you need. On 7–9: they provide it with strings attached.

moves.name: Live from the Dog Park
moves.description: When you broadcast live from a dangerous location, your team takes +1 forward for the scene. You attract extra attention.

moves.name: Something Different
moves.description: Take a move from another team playbook.

moves.name: Change Team Style
moves.description: Pick a new team style.

mandatory.name: The Station
mandatory.description: Your broadcast facilities and the community that gathers around them. Functions as a headquarters and a way to reach your audience directly.

options.name: Headquarters
options.description: A separate base of operations from the station itself.

options.name: Tight Security
options.description: The station is hardened against supernatural intrusion.

options.name: Station Archives
options.description: Decades of recordings, including things that shouldn't have been captured on tape.

options.name: Station Archives (expanded)
options.description: More archive material, or the ability to search it effectively.

options.name: Housing
options.description: Staff accommodation.

options.name: Signal Boosters
options.description: Extended range for your broadcasts.

options.name: Paranatural Boosters
options.description: Your signal can now reach supernatural entities on other planes.

options.name: Station Vans
options.description: Broadcast vehicles for fieldwork.

options.name: Helicopter
options.description: An aerial vehicle for news-gathering.

options.name: Helicopter (second)
options.description: A second helicopter.

options.name: Shelter
options.description: Emergency facilities for listeners and community members in crisis.

options.name: Custom
options.description: FILL_FROM_SESSION

team_playbooks.name: Swipe to Slay
team_playbooks.description: There's an app. It sends you to monster jobs. Nobody knows who built it. It pays well. The jobs are definitely real.

the_app.note: Define your relationship to the app.
how_you_learned: It just appeared on your phone
You found it in an app store that no longer exists
why_you_started: I needed to do something useful
Someone I know was hurt by a monster
I had skills and this was an outlet

options: The app is run by a supernatural entity
The jobs are tests of some kind
Someone is watching us through it
The payments come from nowhere legal
The app has a hidden agenda
There are other users we've never met

styles.name: Side Racket
styles.description: This is gig work. You have a real life too.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we complete the job without it bleeding into our regular lives?

styles.name: Already Doing It
styles.description: You were hunting before the app. This just made it easier.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we use our experience to handle something the app didn't prepare us for?

styles.name: Can't Escape
styles.description: You tried to quit. The app keeps sending jobs. You keep taking them.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did someone try to quit — even unsuccessfully?

styles.name: Fight the Power
styles.description: You're using the app while trying to figure out who controls it.
styles.end_of_session_question: Did we get closer to the truth about who runs the app?

styles.name: Custom
styles.description: FILL_FROM_SESSION
styles.end_of_session_question: FILL_FROM_SESSION

enemies: All Faerie (they hate gig economy logic applied to supernatural contracts)
An evil rival app run by something that wants your clients dead
The Crimson Key, a secret society that pre-dates the app and resents it
The app itself, if it's actually alive
An ancient prophecy the app keeps accidentally fulfilling
Saunders Enterprises, a corporation that wants to buy or destroy the app
Custom: FILL_FROM_SESSION

allies.name: Nak Shimma
allies.description: A dispatcher of some kind — you talk to them through the app but have never met them.

allies.name: "The Derision of the Crest's Edge"
allies.description: A slime demon who reviews your work on the app with unfair ratings but occasionally gives useful tips.

allies.name: Alamo Rick
allies.description: A retired hunter who has opinions about the app and is weirdly helpful anyway.

allies.name: Brother W. Adisu
allies.description: A monastic scholar who tracks supernatural job requests as part of a larger theological project.

allies.name: Sketch and Sykoking
allies.description: Two other users of the app. You've never met in person. They might be more experienced than you.

allies.name: Frank Keep
allies.description: A fixer who handles payments, logistics, and occasionally shows up at jobs uninvited.

allies.name: Custom
allies.description: FILL_FROM_SESSION

moves.name: Thanks for the Rating
moves.description: When a client is satisfied with the outcome, mark team experience. When a client makes things worse despite your best efforts, you take +1 forward dealing with the resulting mess.

moves.name: Chekhov's Incident
moves.description: When a previous job's loose end comes back up, you may ask one free question about it from the investigate a mystery list.

moves.name: Substitutions
moves.description: When another team member can't take a job, you may cover for them. Take their stat bonuses for that scene instead of your own.

moves.name: Double Booked
moves.description: Once per session, two jobs collide in a way that actually makes things easier. The Keeper tells you how.

moves.name: Back Channel
moves.description: When you communicate through the app with someone unusual (another user, the dispatcher, something in the app itself), roll +Cool. On 10+: useful information with no complications. On 7–9: useful information with a complication. Miss: something from the app ends up in the wrong hands.

moves.name: Something Different
moves.description: Take a move from another team playbook.

moves.name: Change Team Style
moves.description: Pick a new team style.

options.name: Never Offline
options.description: The app is always available, even in locations with no signal.

options.name: Transportation
options.description: Reliable vehicles for getting to jobs.

options.name: Mobile Base
options.description: A vehicle that functions as a base for longer jobs.

options.name: Mobile Base (expanded)
options.description: A larger or better-equipped mobile base.

options.name: Dimensional Positioning System
options.description: The app can track supernatural locations, not just physical ones.

options.name: Dimensional Positioning System (upgraded)
options.description: More accurate or extended supernatural tracking.

options.name: The CMS
options.description: Access to the app's back-end content management system. Partial. Sometimes.

options.name: Inventory
options.description: The app provides mundane gear for jobs. You have a reliable supply.

options.name: Arcane Inventory
options.description: The app provides supernatural gear for jobs. You have a reliable supply.

options.name: Headquarters
options.description: A fixed base not tied to the app.

options.name: Suspicious Housing
options.description: Accommodation the app provides. You're not sure what it is exactly, but it's comfortable and secure.

options.name: Order History
options.description: Full access to past jobs. Sometimes what you need is in a job you already did.

options.name: Custom
options.description: FILL_FROM_SESSION
